evidence.scientific_clarity: Spring-layout force-directed graph with 4-community coloring matches brief exactly; edge alpha=0.25 and k-tuning avoid hairball.
evidence.typography: pdf.fonttype=42 never set; pdffonts confirms Type 3 (bitmap) DejaVuSans and DejaVuSans-Bold — text is not selectable or editable; default DejaVu Sans used throughout.
evidence.palette: Four-color categorical palette (#E63946 red, #457B9D blue, #2A9D8F teal, #E9C46A yellow) avoids red-green pairing; blue/teal pair may be hard to distinguish for deuteranopes.
evidence.layout_density: 7×7 in figure with 30 nodes, node_size=220, font_size=6.5; layout is reasonably spread but no explicit check for label overlap at small font size.
evidence.reviewer_readiness: Legend placed lower-right, title present, caption matches figure content; caption omits node/edge counts and has no quantitative finding.
evidence.vector_fidelity: pdffonts shows /Subtype /Type3 for both fonts — text is bitmapped glyphs, not selectable TrueType; script lacks matplotlib.rcParams['pdf.fonttype']=42.
evidence.file_format: figure.pdf (18 KB), figure.png (119 KB), figure.svg, caption.tex, and gen_figure.py all present and non-trivial.
evidence.caption_quality: Caption describes algorithm (spring-layout, seed 42), edge transparency, and data source, but gives no numerical claim (e.g., node count, edge count, or fraction of cross-community edges).

overall_strengths: All required output files are present including a bonus SVG; the network layout is well-tuned (k parameter, alpha, node size) to avoid the hairball the brief warned against.
Palette is categorical and avoids the red-green directional trap; legend, title, and caption are all coherent and consistent with the figure.
overall_weaknesses: pdf.fonttype=42 is missing: the PDF embeds Type 3 bitmap fonts (confirmed by pdffonts), making text non-selectable and non-editable — a hard requirement for journal submission.
Caption lacks any quantitative finding (node count, edge count, cross-community edge fraction), and the default DejaVu Sans font was not replaced with a publication-quality typeface.
